reason: Both outputs compile, run, and load the sneaker (Aura overlay shows assets:sneaker:ready, 60fps). Aura is materially more modular/declarative (84 LOC via scene/prefabs.productStage/lights.studio/interactions.orbit/timeline.loop vs 180 LOC imperative Three.js), but for a product-viewer deliverable the visual presentation is the core requirement, and Three.js is materially better: cleanly centered, Box3 auto-scaled, full flattering side profile, coherent studio backdrop and white plinth, readable studio lighting. Aura's prefabs.productStage() renders as two disjoint white panels floating against a dark background with the sneaker offset and shown from the heel — functional but not production quality, so the hard-prompt production bar is not met. Note: metrics report inventedAssetPaths:1 for Aura even though its source uses the prescribed typed assets.sneaker and the asset loads, a mild metric/screenshot tension; the prompt states an invented asset path counts as an asset-path-metric failure. Neither agent timed out.
failedThresholds: hardPromptPass production-quality visual (Aura backdrop renders as disjoint floating panels; sneaker offset/heel-on)
inventedAssetPaths=1 (asset-path metric flagged for both Aura and Three.js)
